---
title: "Heng Ji on Event Schema Induction @ NAACL 2022"
description: "Heng Ji at SUKI on event schema induction via graph generation, weak supervision via Wikipedia, and PathLM that even predicts the future."
author: "synesis"
date: "2022-07-14"
categories: [NLP, NLU, knowledge graphs, conference, NAACL]
image: cover.jpg
---

::: {layout-ncol=2}
![SUKI Workshop @ NAACL 2022. Image from [SUKI workshop](https://suki-workshop.github.io/).](cover.jpg)

![Slide from Ji's talk at the [SUKI workshop](https://suki-workshop.github.io/).](02.jpg)
:::
Professor Heng Ji (University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign) gave an invited talk at SUKI Workshop @ NAACL2022 on their fascinating work on event schema induction!

(cf. Roger Schank & Robert Abelson. Scripts, Plans, Goals, and Understanding: An Inquiry Into Human Knowledge Structures. 1977)

* Schema induction via graph generation.
* Clever way of weak supervision via Wikipedia.
* The resulting PathLM proves to be improving event IE tasks.
* It can even predict the future on instance graph! (Precrime? :-) )

SUKI Workshop @ NAACL2022: <https://suki-workshop.github.io/>


*Originally posted on [LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/benjaminhan_naacl2022-naacl2022-knowledgegraph-activity-6953574089001177088-RnIq).*
